text: Imidazolium Perchlorate - A molecular ferroelectric thin film on silicon substrate demonstrating high piezoelectric response
intermolecular_interactions: Hydrogen bonding between imidazolium cations and perchlorate anions
experimental_method: Piezoelectric force microscopy (PFM), Second harmonic generation (SHG), Polarization-electric field (P-E) measurements
shear_qualitative: High piezoelectric response observed in thin films
shear_unit: pm/V (inverse piezoelectric coefficient for thin film)
longitudinal_unit: pC/N (single crystal d33 at room temperature)
dft_shear_qualitative: Phase-field simulations confirm domain switching mechanisms
property_ref_title: A molecular ferroelectric thin film of imidazolium perchlorate on silicon

additional_properties.thin_film_preparation: Sol-gel spin-coating with sodium alginate assistance

notes: Experimental crystal structure obtained from the Cambridge Structural Database (CSD)
Deposited CIF corresponds to perdeuteroimidazolium perchlorate (C3D5N2ClO4), crystallographically equivalent to hydrogen analogue
2024 Nature Communications paper experimentally characterizes ferroelectric and piezoelectric properties without depositing new crystal structure
2014 CSD structure (YOCVAP01) is appropriate crystallographic reference for material used in piezoelectric study
Crystal is non-centrosymmetric (R3m, No. 160), crystallographically compatible with piezoelectricity
Organic molecular salt composed of discrete imidazolium cations and perchlorate anions
Single crystals synthesized using facile solution-evaporation method
High-quality highly-oriented thin films grown on Si substrates using SA-assisted spin-coating
